frameworks.name: perceptual inflation
frameworks.one_line: Observers overestimate peripheral perceptual capacity beyond what content supports

frameworks.name: filling-in
frameworks.one_line: Endogenous completion of perceptual content; distinguished from inflation

frameworks.name: metacognition / confidence-accuracy tracking
frameworks.one_line: Degree to which confidence judgements track task accuracy

frameworks.name: signal-detection style decisional bias
frameworks.one_line: Biases in detection (false alarms) probe peripheral introspection

core_question: Does peripheral phenomenology reflect filling-in of content, or inflated introspection about weak content?
key_assumptions: Subjective perceptual richness can dissociate from objective perceptual content
Crowding and summary statistics expose unique peripheral processing limits
Metacognitive sensitivity and decisional bias indexes introspection quality
manipulations: Peripheral vs central viewing using crowding paradigm
Peripheral vs central viewing using summary-statistics paradigm
key_findings: Observers showed overconfidence in peripheral discrimination judgements
Observers showed high false-alarm rates in peripheral detection judgements
Metacognitive deficit and decisional bias both present in peripheral vision
interpretations: Peripheral phenomenology is characterized by inflation, not just filling-in
Inflation reflects incorrect introspection rather than endogenous content generation
